title: Construction project managers and related professionals
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate, usually through subordinate supervisory personnel, activities concerned with the construction and maintenance of structures, facilities, and systems. Participate in the conceptual development of a construction project and oversee its organization, scheduling, budgeting, and implementation. Includes managers in specialized construction fields, such as carpentry or plumbing. Perform engineering duties in planning, designing, and overseeing construction and maintenance of building structures and facilities, such as roads, railroads, airports, bridges, harbors, channels, dams, irrigation projects, pipelines, power plants, and water and sewage systems. Develop plans for surface transportation projects, according to established engineering standards and state or federal construction policy. Prepare designs, specifications, or estimates for transportation facilities. Plan modifications of existing streets, highways, or freeways to improve traffic flow. (Based on 11 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Evaluate proposals for infrastructure projects for environmental impact or sustainability, and review and evaluate environmental impact reports pertaining to planning projects.
Keep informed about economic or legal issues involved in zoning codes, building codes, and environmental regulations, and determine the effects of regulatory limitations on land use projects.
Advocate sustainability to community groups or government agencies and identify opportunities or develop plans for sustainability projects to improve energy efficiency and minimize waste.
Hold public meetings with government officials, developers, and the public to address issues regarding land use or community plans.
Assess the feasibility of land use proposals and identify necessary changes, and investigate property availability for development.
Supervise or coordinate the work of urban planning technicians or technologists, and direct and supervise construction workers and related activities.
Coordinate work with economic consultants or architects during the formulation of plans and prepare designs, specifications, or estimates for infrastructure projects.
Discuss land use project purposes with planning officials and prepare, maintain, and update files, including land use data and statistics.
Conduct field investigations, surveys, or impact studies to compile and analyze data on economic, social, regulatory, or physical factors affecting development.
Design and administer government plans related to land use and zoning, and implement environmental protection programs and identify risks for civil engineering projects.
Prepare and submit budget estimates and progress reports, and develop construction budgets comparing green and non-green alternatives.
Inspect projects to monitor compliance with building and safety codes or environmental regulations, including conducting pre-building assessments.
Plan, organize, or direct activities concerned with the construction or maintenance of structures and systems, and plan project activities to meet deadlines.
Issue permits for construction or occupancy, and apply for necessary permits or licenses during project planning.
Prepare contracts and negotiate revisions with contractors and subcontractors, including preparing administrative or technical reports on various project matters.
Conduct hydrological analyses for water resources planning and investigate traffic problems, recommending improvements.
Perform hydraulic analyses and evaluate the operation and maintenance of water systems to improve efficiency.
Inspect completed projects to ensure compliance with safety, environmental, or governmental regulations and monitor mapping work for accuracy.
Provide technical direction or supervision to junior engineers and technicians, and present data or information at construction-related public hearings.
Design water distribution systems, treatment plants, and other water-related infrastructure, ensuring compliance with standards.
Conduct feasibility studies for the construction of water supply systems and analyze environmental impact statements for transportation projects.
Gather and analyze water use data to forecast demand and evaluate construction project materials for compliance with environmental standards.
Prepare project budgets, schedules, and specifications, and conduct studies of traffic patterns to assess potential impacts of projects.
Review and interpret construction plans and specifications to ensure compliance with regulations, including issuing approvals based on these reviews.
Inspect bridges, dams, and other structures during construction for safety and compliance with codes, including verifying dimensions and alignments.
Model transportation scenarios to evaluate the impacts of new developments and conduct water quality studies related to pollutant sources.

Conduct surveys to ascertain the locations of natural features and man-made structures on the Earth's surface, underground, and underwater, using electronic distance-measuring equipment, such as GPS, and other surveying instruments. Collect information needed to carry out new surveys, using source maps, previous survey data, photographs, computer records, or other relevant information. Adjust and operate surveying instruments such as prisms, theodolites, electronic distance measuring equipment, or electronic data collectors. Monitor installation of plumbing, wiring, equipment, or appliances to ensure that installation is performed properly and is in compliance with applicable regulations.

Prepare job specification sheets and produce or update overlay maps to show information boundaries, water locations, or topographic features on various base maps or at different scales. Analyze proposed site factors and design maps, graphs, tracings, and diagrams to illustrate findings. Read and review project blueprints and structural specifications to determine dimensions of structure or system and material requirements. Draft detailed dimensional drawings and design layouts for projects to ensure conformance to specifications. Prepare reports and document project activities and data. Conduct inspections using survey instruments, metering devices, tape measures, or test equipment. Inspect project site and evaluate contractor work to detect design malfunctions and ensure conformance to design specifications and applicable codes.

Train, direct, or supervise other construction inspectors and confer with supervisors to determine project details such as plan preparation, acceptance testing, and evaluation of field conditions. Identify and prioritize energy-saving measures. Analyze technical feasibility of energy-saving measures using knowledge of engineering, energy use, construction, maintenance, system operation, or process systems. Report maintenance problems occurring at project sites to supervisors and negotiate changes to resolve system conflicts.

Calculate dimensions, square footage, profile and component specifications, and material quantities, using calculators or computers. Quantify energy consumption to establish baselines for energy use or need. Verify income eligibility of participants in publicly financed weatherization programs. Conduct environmental hazard inspections to identify or quantify problems, such as asbestos, poor air quality, water contamination, or other environmental hazards. Inspect newly installed energy-efficient equipment to ensure that it was installed properly and is performing according to specifications.

Prepare designs, specifications, or estimates for transportation facilities. Plan modifications of existing streets, highways, or freeways to improve traffic flow. Evaluate project details to ensure adherence to environmental regulations.

Inspect buildings to locate hazardous conditions and fire code violations. Analyze evidence and other information to determine the probable cause of fire or explosion. Conduct inspections and acceptance testing of newly installed fire protection systems. Communicate fire details to superiors, subordinates, or interagency dispatch centers, using two-way radios. Recommend equipment modifications or new equipment purchases. Recommend changes to fire prevention, inspection, and fire code endorsement procedures.

Monitor construction activities to ensure that environmental regulations are not violated, and assess the nature and extent of issues related to energy and environmental sustainability. Supervise staff, training them, planning their work, and evaluating their performance. Attend training classes to maintain current knowledge of fire prevention, safety, and firefighting procedures.

Respond to public suggestions and complaints, and provide assistance in the development of methods and procedures for conducting field surveys, planning and conducting field surveys to locate new sites and analyze details of project sites.
